fields.description:
## Summary
--exclude-deleted exists on the CLI only; the library and MCP list_tickets cannot exclude deleted tickets — an interface-parity gap.

## Reproduction Steps
(verified on installed 0.4.0 == HEAD, temp store)
1. CLI: rebar list --include-archived --exclude-deleted -> works (deleted dropped).
2. Library: inspect.signature(rebar.list_tickets) -> no exclude_deleted parameter; MCP list_tickets likewise.
3. _reads.list_tickets hardcodes exclude_deleted=False.

## Expected vs Actual
- Expected: the three interfaces expose the same list filters (parity is the project's stated contract).
- Actual: library/MCP callers cannot express the filter at all.

## Source
- src/rebar/_reads.py:94-96 (hardcoded False)
- src/rebar/__init__.py:300-322; src/rebar/mcp_server.py:145-167 (missing kwarg)
- CLI flag: src/rebar/_engine/rebar:89

## Fix direction
Thread exclude_deleted: bool = False through _reads.list_tickets -> library list_tickets -> MCP tool; add the missing parity case to tests/interfaces (the suite does not cover this flag today — close that coverage gap with the fix).

## Suggested RED test
Same store containing a deleted ticket; all three interfaces with the flag on and off -> identical results (CLI is the oracle).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] exclude_deleted available on library + MCP, default matching current behavior (False)
- [ ] Interface-parity test covers the flag (RED first)
- [ ] list_tickets outputSchema/docs updated
